title: trifolium fragiferum subsp. tumens
intro: Trifolium fragiferum subsp. tumens is a plant species, also known as white clover, which is a perennial legume native to Europe. The medicinal properties of Trifolium fragiferum subsp. tumens are attributed to its isoflavones and other bioactive compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues, reduce cholesterol levels, and support cardiovascular health. Trifolium fragiferum subsp. tumens can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as dried flowers and leaves that can be used in teas, capsules, or as a dietary supplement.
